title: Chromolaena sanctopaulensis
taxonomy: Chromolaena sanctopaulensis is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Chromolaena, which is a group of plants commonly known as the yellowheads. The genus Chromolaena is further classified under the tribe Eupatorieae, which includes various species of flowering plants native to the Americas. Chromolaena sanctopaulensis is native to Brazil and is a relatively rare species within its genus. By classification, it is a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its germinated seedling stage.
uses: Chromolaena sanctopaulensis, a plant species native to Brazil, has been found to possess various medicinal properties. Its leaves and stems are used in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. side_effects: Chromolaena sanctopaulensis, also known as the Devil's nettle, contains compounds that may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and other adverse effects in some individuals.
